figures.caption: Figure B.14: The prompt of the LLM in IntenBot.
Figure 7: The subjective result of the XR experience study.

Figure 9: Four interaction scenarios in a meeting room for demonstration. Non-voice input, using finger-pointing, sends the robot back to its charging dock (upper). The implicit voice command “Check”, combined with gaze and finger-pointing input, directs the robot to check the bag (middle-upper). The explicit command “Is TV on?”, together with gaze and finger-pointing input, instructs the robot to check whether the TV is on (middle-lower). The implicit command “Come back” and gaze input trigger the robot to return (lower).
Figure 2: The flow of the IntenBot system.
Figure 3: The VR home-like scene for the user behavior study (left). The Stroop test condition (middle). The task reminder and the mini-map with the marked target object(s) (right).
Figure A.13: User input time for each attempt (left) and percentage of high priority for each attempt (right) in XR experience study.

Figure 5: We began by evaluating the accuracy of various combinations of gaze and fingerpointing angle ranges to identify the optimal pair for use in the (E) and (I) interaction methods. In the initial rough search (Bottom), we tested angle ranges from 2◦ to 32◦, with 3◦ increments for both gaze and pointing simultaneously. In the refined analysis (Top), we examined specific combinations of (gaze angle range, finger-pointing angle range) within these intervals, again using a 3◦ step size. Finally, two peaks at (11◦, 14◦) (90.13%) and (14◦, 11◦)(90.46%) are identified.
Figure 6: The objective results of the XR experience study.
Figure 8: The flowchart of the robot planning module.

Figure 1: IntenBot allows users to use flexible and imprecise multimodal input for casual and human-like interaction with robots. Users can use implicit voice input “Bring me that.” combined with gaze and finger-pointing input to issue a command (left). The thumb touches and presses a ring to activate the system and handle multimodal input timing (middle-left). IntenBot leverages LLMs to disambiguate flexible and imprecise multimodal input, interpret user intentions, and generate potential instructions for user confirmation (middle-right). The robot then executes the task of bringing the cola (right).
Figure B.15: The prompt of the scene object information.
